hero.name: Guilherme Narciso
hero.tagline: Backend specialist in Java & Go · Microservices · Cloud (AWS & Azure) · 3+ years at Accenture & Netcracker
about.title: About Me
about.p1: I'm a mid-level Software Engineer with 3+ years of experience building large-scale distributed systems. I specialize in Java (Spring Boot) and Go, with hands-on experience in microservices architecture, cloud-native environments, and event-driven systems.
about.p2: At Accenture, I worked on Brazil's leading pharmacy marketplace (RaiaDrogasil, 47.6M customers) and built real-time socket integrations for Circle K's fuel pump network across 29 countries. Currently at Netcracker, I develop and maintain the NDO (Network Domain Orchestration) product for Tier 1 global operators like Google Fiber, T-Mobile, Rogers, Telesat, and Swisscom.
about.p3: I'm passionate about solving hard engineering problems — from optimizing a production query from 40s to 6s on 2M+ records, to designing event-driven architectures that bridge hardware and cloud systems.
experience.title: Experience
experience.netcracker.bullets.0: Member of the first Brazilian team for Netcracker's NDO (Network Domain Orchestration) OSS product — a NEC subsidiary with ~$2.7B revenue — developing features and fixing bugs for global Tier 1 operators: Google Fiber, T-Mobile, Rogers, Telesat, and Swisscom.
experience.netcracker.bullets.1: Resolved a critical production timeout on Google Fiber's go-live: traced the bottleneck via Graylog, profiled and re-indexed a query in ArangoDB, reducing execution time from 40s+ to 6s with 2M+ active records.
experience.netcracker.bullets.2: Backend development with Java (Spring Boot) and Go, unit testing via JUnit and Mockito, Docker, Kubernetes, Kafka, and CI/CD pipelines in an Agile Scrum team.
experience.accenture.raiadrogasil.bullet: Delivered features for Brazil's leading pharmacy marketplace (47.6M active customers) using Spring Boot microservices, Next.js, RabbitMQ, and OracleDB, with observability via Datadog.
Developed the Click & Collect feature (buy online, pick up in store), integrated with inventory systems via microservices across the marketplace catalog.
Built a BFF in Node.js/NestJS with GraphQL and MongoDB caching, reducing latency by avoiding direct calls to third-party providers.
experience.accenture.circlek.bullets.0: Built a Java socket server for real-time communication with fuel pumps at Circle K (Couche-Tard — 17,000+ stores in 29 countries), implementing an adapter pattern for 4+ pump models.
experience.accenture.circlek.bullets.1: Designed an event orchestrator on cloud-native Azure (CosmosDB, Service Bus, AKS) with Azure Monitor observability to coordinate pump events across the system.
